learningObjectives: Name and use twelve environment and nature words at Flyers A2 level.
Understand simple passive forms: is made, are recycled.
Use must and mustn't to talk about eco rules and responsibilities.
Read a short text about an eco project and identify text type and purpose.
Listen for several facts in dialogues about an eco club and community cleanup.
Write a short message (at least 25 words) about protecting nature or recycling.
Talk about pollution, recycling and rules in extended spoken answers.
grammarFocus: Passive voice (introduction): is made, is recycled

grammarReference.structure: Passive voice (introduction): is made / are made
grammarReference.explanation: Use is/are + past participle to say what happens to something. The plastic is made into new bins. Paper is made from old newspapers. The focus is on the thing, not who does the action.
examples: New bins are made from recycled plastic.
Paper is made from old newspapers.
The report is written by our teacher.
Trees are planted near the playground.
commonMistakes: Plastic is make into bins (×) → Plastic is made into bins (✓)
Paper are made from trees (×) → Paper is made from trees (✓)
The bins is made yesterday (×) → The bins were made yesterday (✓) — past needs were

grammarReference.structure: Passive voice: is recycled / are recycled
grammarReference.explanation: Use is recycled or are recycled to talk about waste and materials. Plastic bottles are recycled every week. Water is not recycled at home in the same way. Plural nouns use are recycled.
examples: Plastic is recycled at our school.
Old bottles are recycled on Fridays.
Glass is recycled into new jars.
Food waste is not recycled here yet.
commonMistakes: Plastic is recycle at school (×) → Plastic is recycled at school (✓)
The bottles is recycled (×) → The bottles are recycled (✓)
Paper recycled every day (×) → Paper is recycled every day (✓) — need is

grammarReference.explanation: Use must + base verb for important rules: We must protect nature. Use mustn't to say something is not allowed: You mustn't drop plastic in the ocean. Must comes before the verb without to.
examples: We must put rubbish in the right bins.
You mustn't throw waste on the ground.
Students must wear gloves during cleanup.
commonMistakes: We must to recycle paper (×) → We must recycle paper (✓)
You mustn't to drop plastic (×) → You mustn't drop plastic (✓)
We must protects nature (×) → We must protect nature (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Read for Gist

exercises.title: Learn: Minh's Eco Project
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Answer the questions.

passage.title: Minh's School Eco Project
passage.text:
Minh is eleven and lives in Hanoi. This term his school started an eco project to protect nature and keep the environment clean. Minh and his friend Linh joined the school eco club.

Every Friday the club collects plastic waste from classrooms. The plastic is recycled and new bins are made from it. Old paper is recycled too. Their teacher says we must sort waste carefully and we mustn't throw rubbish on the ground.

Minh wrote a short article for the school board. It describes why rainforests and oceans on our planet are important. Pollution hurts animals in the ocean. The article asks everyone to help.

On Sunday Mum took Minh and Linh to a community cleanup near West Lake. They filled three bags with waste. Minh felt proud because small actions can protect the earth.

lines.text: Welcome to the eco club. Today we must plan our recycling project.

lines.text: How much plastic waste did we collect last week?

lines.text: We collected twenty bottles. They are recycled on Fridays.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Paper is made from old newspapers, isn't it?

lines.text: We mustn't drop plastic in the ocean. Pollution hurts fish.

lines.speaker: Teacher

lines.text: Thanks for helping with the community cleanup, Minh and Linh.

lines.text: Look — there is a lot of plastic waste near the lake.

lines.text: We must put it in these bags. The earth must stay clean.

lines.text: You mustn't leave sharp bottles on the ground.

lines.text: This plastic is recycled at our school, Mum.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: We filled two bags already. One more and we finish!

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: I'm proud of you. Nature looks better already.

lines.text: Small actions protect our planet for the future.

script.title: Eco Club Meeting
script.setting: School classroom after lessons, eco club meeting

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Welcome to the eco club. Today we must plan our recycling project.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: How much plastic waste did we collect last week?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: We collected twenty bottles. They are recycled on Fridays.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Paper is made from old newspapers, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Yes. New paper is made from paper you bring from home.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: We must protect rainforests and oceans on our planet.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: We mustn't drop plastic in the ocean. Pollution hurts fish.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good. Next Friday we will plant trees near the playground.

content.taskDescription: Write a short message to your English teacher about Minh's school eco project. Say what the club collects, what is recycled, and one rule using must or mustn't.

modelAnswer.text: Our eco club collects plastic waste every Friday. The plastic is recycled and new bins are made from it. Paper is recycled too. We must sort waste carefully. We mustn't throw rubbish on the ground because pollution hurts nature.
